description: Poke-mode sensors hold worker slots for hours and deadlock the scheduler under concurrency.
body: Failure mode: many sensors run in poke mode, each occupying a worker slot while polling for hours, so the executor's slots fill with waiting sensors and no real tasks can run, stalling the whole scheduler. Detection signals: a worker pool saturated by sensor tasks in a running state, and ready downstream tasks queued behind them unable to start. Do not just add more workers to absorb the waiting sensors. Safer fallback: switch sensors to reschedule or deferrable mode so waiting frees the slot, set explicit timeouts, and limit concurrent sensors, then confirm real tasks can schedule while sensors wait.
